Question:
                                When did I book the Airbnb in Sacramento?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/25 (Thu) 14:37

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                The information provided is not enough. You only mentioned booking Airbnb in San Francisco.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:982b5123_abs
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-13587db590b0-claim-7 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user attended their best friend's wedding in Haight-Ashbury and stayed there after booking lodging three months in advance.; scope=User's past travel and social-event history.; context="The user described a past Haight-Ashbury Airbnb stay for their best friend's wedding."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 quote="when I stayed in Haight-Ashbury for my best friend's wedding and had to book three months in advance"
- claim hot-path-13587db590b0-claim-10 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user will probably take BART and then a short taxi ride to their Airbnb in Haight-Ashbury from SFO Airport.; scope=Transportation plan for the user's San Francisco trip from SFO Airport to the Haight-Ashbury Airbnb.; context="The user selected a likely airport-to-Airbnb transportation plan after receiving options."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 quote="I'll probably take BART and then a short taxi ride to my Airbnb"
- claim hot-path-13587db590b0-claim-6 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has had a great past experience with Airbnb.; scope=User's lodging-service experience relevant to travel recommendations.; context="The user mentioned using Airbnb while staying in Haight-Ashbury for their best friend's wedding."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 quote="I've had a great experience with Airbnb in the past"
- claim hot-path-13587db590b0-claim-5 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user is planning a trip to San Francisco.; scope=User's San Francisco trip planning context from the imported 2023-05-25 chat session.; context="In a past chat session dated 2023-05-25, the user asked for neighborhood recommendations for an upcoming San Francisco trip."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 quote="I'm planning a trip to San Francisco"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-13587db590b0-note-3 [PLAN/ACTIVE]: Haight-Ashbury no-car transport approach; For the Haight-Ashbury Airbnb stay, the user expected to avoid renting a car and favored taking BART from SFO followed by a short taxi ride to the Airbnb.; scope=Airport-to-Airbnb transportation plan for the user's San Francisco/Haight-Ashbury trip; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 quote="I don't plan on renting a car"
- note hot-path-79d0f7794797-note-2 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: San Francisco and Bay Area road-trip planning context; The user's 2023-05-25 travel-planning context combined an upcoming San Francisco trip with a changed intention to rent a car, interest in Haight-Ashbury breakfast at The Pork Store Cafe, and Bay Area scenic drives such as Pacific Coast Highway, Muir Woods/Sausalito, Napa Valley, Skyline Boulevard, and Mount Tamalpais.; scope=User's San Francisco and Bay Area travel planning context from the 2023-05-25 session; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:4b0cb668852c5874f92a787fb3dfd9d6 quote="I'm actually planning to rent a car for my upcoming San Francisco trip"

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:a99109b1d9b05ea5d855ea9a4791e7f4 [external_record ref=longmemeval:982b5123_abs:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/25 (Thu) 03:03
Transcript:
user: I'm planning a trip to San Francisco and was wondering if you could recommend some good neighborhoods to stay in. By the way, I've had a great experience with Airbnb in the past, like when I stayed in Haight-Ashbury for my best friend's wedding and had to book three months in advance.
assistant: San Francisco is an amazing city! I'd be happy to help you find a great neighborhood to stay in.

First, I have to say that Haight-Ashbury is a fantastic choice, especially if you're looking for a vibrant and eclectic atmosphere. However, there are many other neighborhoods in San Francisco that might suit your taste and preferences. Since you've had a good experience with Airbnb, I'll suggest some popular neighborhoods to consider:...
...2. **North Beach**: This charming neighborhood is known for its Italian-American culture, delicious food, and lively atmosphere. It's within walking distance to Fisherman's Wharf and has plenty of Airbnb options....
...When booking your Airbnb, be sure to check the neighborhood, proximity to public transportation, and reviews from previous guests to ensure you find the perfect spot for your stay.

Remember to book in advance, especially during peak season (June to August), as San Francisco can get quite busy!...
...It's essential to book an appointment well in advance, especially during peak season, to ensure that your suit is ready in time for the wedding. Be prepared to spend around 2-3 fittings to get the perfect fit....
...Remember to plan ahead, and don't hesitate to ask for recommendations or advice from the tailors themselves. They'll be able to guide you through the process and help you look your best for the wedding!
user: I'll definitely check out those options. Can you also help me with transportation from the airport to my Airbnb in the city? I don't plan on renting a car.
assistant: San Francisco has a well-connected airport and a comprehensive public transportation system, making it easy to get to your Airbnb without a car. Here are some options to consider:

1. **BART (Bay Area Rapid Transit)**: This is a convenient and affordable way to get to the city from SFO Airport. Take the BART train from the airport to the Civic Center Station, which is in the heart of San Francisco. From there, you can take a short taxi or ride-hail ride (Uber/Lyft) to your Airbnb. The trip takes around 30-40 minutes, and the fare is approximately $9.65.
2. **SuperShuttle**: This is a shared-ride van service that can take you from the airport to your Airbnb. You can book online in advance, and the fare is around $17-20 per person, depending on the location. The trip takes around 30-40 minutes, depending on traffic....
...4. **Ride-hailing (Uber/Lyft)**: You can use ride-hailing services to get from the airport to your Airbnb. The fare will depend on the time of day, traffic, and the type of vehicle you choose. Expect to pay around $30-40, depending on the circumstances.
5. **Private car services**: If you prefer a more luxurious and hassle-free experience, you can book a private car service to take you from the airport to your Airbnb. This option is more expensive, with fares starting at around $60-70.

Once you arrive at your Airbnb, you can use public transportation, walk, or take ride-hailing services to get around the city. San Francisco has a comprehensive network of buses, trains, and cable cars, making it easy to navigate without a car.

Remember to check with your Airbnb host for any specific transportation recommendations or tips for getting to their location. They might have some insider knowledge to make your trip smoother!...
...When visiting these shops, be sure to ask about their gift basket services and let them know your budget and preferences. They'll be happy to help you create a custom basket that fits your needs....
- source external:provided-text:4b0cb668852c5874f92a787fb3dfd9d6 [external_record ref=longmemeval:982b5123_abs:session:2 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/25 (Thu) 10:17
Transcript:
user: I'm planning a trip to San Francisco for next month and I was wondering if you could recommend some good restaurants in the Haight-Ashbury neighborhood. By the way, I've been to SF before, exactly two months ago, for my best friend's wedding - it was a 5-day trip and I had an amazing time....
...5. **Magnolia Gastropub**: Another popular spot for craft beer enthusiasts, Magnolia serves up tasty pub fare, including burgers, sandwiches, and salads....
...assistant: Your new navy blue suit sounds sharp! I'm happy to help you with your question, but I have to clarify that I'm a large language model, I don't have real-time access to specific tailor shops or their inventory in San Francisco....
...1. **Tent:** Look for a waterproof, sturdy, and spacious tent that can accommodate your family comfortably. Consider a tent with a rain fly, good ventilation, and easy setup....
...3. **Plan Meals:** Prepare meals in advance, and consider bringing a cooler for perishable items....
...I hope these tips and suggestions help you plan a comfortable and enjoyable camping trip!
user: I'm actually planning to rent a car for my upcoming San Francisco trip, so I was wondering if you could recommend any scenic drives or routes that I shouldn't miss around the Bay Area....
...user: That sounds great! I'm really excited to explore the Bay Area. By the way, speaking of road trips, I recall that about six weeks ago, I drove to my sister's place, which is about 2 hours away from my hometown, to help her move into her new apartment. We spent the entire day packing and unpacking boxes, and in the evening, we ordered pizza and watched a movie together. I remember that I had to take a detour on my way back due to a road closure caused by a landslide. Do you have any tips on how to prepare for road closures or unexpected detours during a road trip?...
...2. **Plan Your Route:** Map out your route in advance and consider alternative routes in case of a road closure.
3. **Download a GPS or Mapping App:** Use a GPS device or a mapping app like Waze or Google Maps that can provide real-time traffic updates and suggest alternative routes.

**During Your Trip:**

1. **Stay Informed:** Keep a radio or a phone nearby to stay informed about road closures or traffic updates....
...3. **Take Breaks:** Take regular breaks to rest, stretch, and check for traffic updates....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [LOCATION]: Haight-Ashbury; Location named Haight-Ashbury.
- entity entity:<1> [LOCATION]: San Francisco International Airport; Location named San Francisco International Airport.
- entity entity:<2> [LOCATION]: San Francisco; Location named San Francisco.
- entity entity:<3> [SERVICE]: Airbnb; Service named Airbnb.
- entity entity:<4> [SERVICE]: BART; Service named BART.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

structured.fullText: {"hypothesis":"There isn't enough information to say when you booked an Airbnb in Sacramento; the retrieved memories only mention an Airbnb stay in Haight-Ashbury/San Francisco booked three months in advance.","reason":"The context contains Airbnb booking information for San Francisco/Haight-Ashbury but no Sacramento Airbnb booking.","supported":true}
